Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 3 SSE Woodson. Estimated 80 mph winds based on NWS storm survey. Damage to roof of main residence as well as damage to metal roof barn on property. In addition, a small trailer rolled over on its side. Time of damage based on radar data.

Estimated 80 mph winds based on NWS storm survey. Damage to roof of main residence as well as damage to metal roof barn on property. In addition, a small trailer rolled over on its side. Time of damage based on radar data.
71 mph wind gust at the west texas mesonet site west of Rotan.

Estimated 85 mph winds based on NWS storm survey. Damage to door and roof of large metal barn. In addition, large tree branches were broken off several trees. Also, a/c compressor on side of house moved off its foundation and flipped on its side. Time of damage estimated by radar.

Brief tornado occurred that damaged numerous trees, and uprooted at least two trees. The tornado also blew off many roof shingles from a house, uplifted metal roofing panels from a small outbuilding, moved a metal carport at least 15 feet, moved and blew over a trash dumpster, and pushed a trailer a few feet off its parked location on a stationary block. Estimated max width of damage path of 100 yards.

Delayed report. 199 foot tower at the KTXS TV station was bent and pushed over onto the roof and all the way to the other side of the property, while the base of the tower stayed intact. Meanwhile, in the rear of the KTXS TV station facility, a large, heavy air conditioning unit on top of the garage being blown over was likely the cause of the roof caving in and partial collapse of the exterior brick walls of the garage. Finally, the roof of the carport in the rear of the KTXS TV station was blown off, with one panel of the roof being sent to the other side of the building. Time estimated from radar.
multiple branches down. Fence knocked down.
78 mph wind gust at the West Texas mesonet location on the ACU campus.

Delayed Report: Received photos of water over FM 605 and 707 this morning on social media. Some portions of the road had water around 2 ft deep. Estimated time of peak flooding last night using MRMS data.

Delayed report. Following damage occurred at a 1 family residence: portion of a gutter peeled off, debris blown up against fence and in the yard, shed blown over upside down, solar panel installation pulled slightly out of the ground, and large tree branches knocked down. Time estimated based off of radar.
Report from mPING: Street/road flooding; Street/road closed; Vehicles stranded.

An off duty NWS employee measured hail stones of 3.25 inches. A local resident reported even larger hail with at least 25 pickup trucks and larger trucks that had windows broken and significant denting. One pickup truck had such a huge dent that the engine stopped working. At least 3 people sustained large bruises and a knee injury from being hit by the hail.
Delayed report. Public reported baseball size hail near the intersection of US 190 and FM 1828. Time estimated based on radar.
Corrects previous hail report from Haskell.
Portions of a roof were peeled off a church.
There was high water on the roads beginning to flood vehicles. Also, there other reports of flash flooding in Abilene.
Mesonet station 102 5ESE Stamford Stamford/Jones County.
Metal roof blown off a small building across from the Abilene Livestock Auction.
ASOS station KABI Abilene.
Hangar door blown off track at Freeman Jet Center-Abilene Regional Airport.
Billboard completely destroyed and wrapped around a telephone pole.
Brady Fire dept Rescue team called to report that they are pulling people out of flooded homes and trapped vehicles in flood waters.
This is a delayed report from July 4th. The town of Streeter was flooded. The time of the report was estimated by radar.
Law Enforcement Dispatch reported flooding along 87 near FM2105, FM2288, in Carlsbad, and in Grape Creek where they currently have Water Rescues ongoing for people in flooded cars.
Report from San Saba Co. dispatch stating that there was a flooded crossing along CR 362 near Dry Creek where the FD could not cross with their fire truck on the way to fight a small house fire.
Photo of a few inches of water flooding a persons home off of US 87 and FM 2105.
Report of several homes flooded in Brady.
Video of flooding at 42nd street and Travis street intersection, with water up to a police mans knees and water getting into a persons house.
This is delayed report from July 4th. Most of the low water crossings and county roads were inundated across Menard County. Also, there were several water rescues. The time is estimated by radar.
Video of home on FM 2105 with at least a foot of water inside it.
Video of a couple feet of flowing flood waters with scattered debris/damage off of 50th street and Armstrong street.
Reports and photos of flooded homes in the Paulann area of San Angelo.
Video of a yard completely flooded in Grape Creek. At least a foot deep.
Video of flooding on North Bell Street, with a car stuck in the flooded waters. Water is up to the top of the tires of the car.
Video of yard flooding almost waist deep.
House displaced and floating down roadway.
Photo of Pulliam street flooded with what looks like at least 1 foot of water.
This is a delayed report from July 4th. US Highway 87 was flooded near Dry Prong Road, north of Mason. The time of report is estimated by radar.
Fire truck fully submerged in flood waters at Loop 306 and Bell Street.

This is a delayed report from July 4th. There were several high water rescues including a boat rescue from a mobile home. One motorist ended up in a tree. Also, low water crossings were inundated. The time of report is estimated by radar.
Large hauling trailer displaced and floating in flooded waters through a neighborhood.
This is a delayed report from July 4th. The highway 29 and 377 split was flooded, west-northwest of Mason. The time of report was estimated by radar.
This is a delayed report from July 4th. There was flooding at a low water crossing along James River Road. The time is estimated by radar.
Swiftwater rescue near the intersection of FM765 and County Road 450.
Water getting into some peoples homes in Menard and the river is over the low water crossing.
The KSJT ASOS at the San Angelo Airport recorded a 59 mph wind gust.
The McCulloch County Sheriff Office reported widespread street flooding in Brady, with a number of road closures.
Report of 2 and a half feet of water over RM 2291 in north-central Kimble county.
Report of a flooded draw on Highway 915 north of Eldorado.
FM 1851 and Highway 71 flooded.
Report of cars stalling on interstate 10 due to road flooding.
On the south side of Sonora, water getting close to flooding some homes.

The Mason County Sheriff Office reported 2 streets significantly flooded with approximately 3 feet of water over the road. In addition, 4 low water crossings in Mason County are flooded with a foot of water over each of those low water crossings.
The Menard County Emergency Manager reported flooding of the low water crossing of Ranch Road 864 and the San Saba River.
The Menard County Emergency Manager reported that a portion of Ranch Road 1221 is closed due to flooding.
Menard County Emergency Manager reported that a portion of Ranch Road 1773 is closed due to flooding.
The Menard County Emergency Manager reported that Ranch to Market Road 1674 is closed due to flooding, in southwestern Menard County.
The Schleicher County Sheriff Office reported that FM 915 road is closed due to flooding, approximately 3.5 miles north of Eldorado.

The Kimble County Emergency Manager reported numerous roads flooded in Kimble County, with road closures at various locations. In addition, numerous streets are flooded in the towns of Junction and Roosevelt.
The Kimble County Emergency Manager reported that streets are flooded in the town of Roosevelt.

The Sutton County Sheriff Office reported flooding and closures on parts the following ranch roads and county roads in Sutton County: 864, 1691, 307, 310 and 311. In addition, streets in the vicinity of the draws in Sonora have flooding.
Some residences are being evacuated in Sonora due to flooding.
Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 2 NNW Sherwood. 58 MPH wind gust measured by the Texas Tech Mesonet site near Mertzon.
58 MPH wind gust measured by the Texas Tech Mesonet site near Mertzon.
This is a delayed report. There were 13 power poles blown down.
Damage to a fence of a residence was reported on social media.
Report of roughly 60 mph wind gusts from a downburst that damaged part of a fence line.
Fence line damage reported from downburst winds, estimated around 60 mph.
Trained spotter reported damaging winds 1 mile to the north of Clyde, TX. He received some damage to his house.

Corrects previous thunderstorm wind damage report from 1 SSW Sonora. The Sutton County Sheriffs Office reported numerous roofs blown off, power poles are down, and numerous large trees are down just south of Sonora off of U.S. HWY 277. The Texas State Operations Center reported that high winds destroyed one house and significantly damaged approximately 20 others. Multiple trees and power lines were blown down, leaving an estimated 24 residents without power.
The Sutton County Sheriffs Office reported numerous roofs blown off, power poles are down, and numerous large trees are down just south of Sonora off of U.S. HWY 277.

Delayed report. NWS storm survey of wind damage to an apartment structure near Sutton County Feed & Supply Store. Metal roofing of the apartment was stripped off from winds associated with a microburst. In addition, a tree with very large branches near the apartment was split in half. Winds were estimated to be 80 to 85 mph based on the damage to the apartment structure and the tree near the apartment.

Delayed report. NWS storm survey of wind damage to a wool warehouse. Significant wind damage to the metal roof of the warehouse. Winds were estimated at 85 mph based on the roof damage to the warehouse.
The Sutton County Emergency Manager reported that multiple low water crossing around the city had 1 to 3 feet of flowing water and barricades were placed at these low water crossings.
Delayed Report. Measured by Brownwood Regional Airport AWOS.
Street flooding was reported in Menard. In addition, flooding was reported in the Harris Hollow section of Menard.
Ranch Road 2291 is closed, due to flooding.
Video was shared on social media, showing extensive flooding in Menard.
Floodwater has started to enter homes in the Harris Hollow section of Menard.
Delayed Report; Near Highway 36 in Eula/Clyde area. Location approximated.
